_comment: Servable models. Paths are relative to the repo root. Add an entry here to
serve a new checkpoint; no server code changes needed as long as its 'kind'
Every entry MUST name the vocab file its checkpoint was trained against.
Token ids carry no meaning on their own, and two vocabs can share a size while
disagreeing on every pattern -- backends.py cross-checks vocab size against the
checkpoint's lm_head, which catches swapped files but NOT a same-size mismatch.
'd_model'/'layers'/'nhead'/'dim_feedforward' must match how the run was trained
(ARTrainConfig defaults: 256/6/8/1024) or load_state_dict will reject the weights.

native_bpe.description: 3D-BPE piece AR, native 32^3 (T18 arm B). Big builds, chunky streaming.
native_bpe.notes: Trained by: python -m blockgen.experiments_native --epochs 60 (arm B). Vocab was not saved by that run; recovered via scripts/rebuild_native_vocab.py.

canon16_flat.description: Per-voxel AR on canon-16 miniatures (T18 arm A). Highest validity; one block per token.
canon16_flat.notes: Same run as native_bpe (arm A). Miniature regime: 16^3 downsampled, so builds are small. NOTE: run_20260715_062404_native is a --quick 2-epoch smoke of the same arms -- its checkpoint is byte-compatible (identical shapes) but undertrained, so a vocab/lm_head check cannot tell them apart. Use this run.

text_cond.description: CLIP-text-conditioned piece AR (T15). Use /gen <text>; bare /gen samples its null branch.
text_cond.notes: Conditioning fidelity is still weak -- palette transfers, geometry mostly does not. Expect a house, not your house.
